Class RequestParser.RequestInfo

java.lang.Object
server.RequestParser.RequestInfo
Enclosing class:
RequestParser
public static class RequestParser.RequestInfo extends Object
Immutable snapshot of a parsed HTTP request.

Instances are created by RequestParser.parseRequest(java.io.BufferedReader) and passed to servlets for request handling. Field values are exposed through getter methods only.

  • Constructor Summary

    RequestInfo(String httpCommand, String uri, String[] uriSegments, Map<String,String> parameters, byte[] content)
    Creates a new request info object with the parsed request data.

    Returns the parameters map extracted from the query string and, in course/test mode, from additional key=value lines after the headers.

    Returns the full request URI as it appeared on the request line.

  • Constructor Details

    • RequestInfo

      public RequestInfo(String httpCommand, String uri, String[] uriSegments, Map<String,String> parameters, byte[] content)
      Creates a new request info object with the parsed request data.
      Parameters:
      httpCommand - the HTTP method from the request line (e.g. "GET")
      uri - the full request URI including the query string, if present
      uriSegments - path segments after the leading slash (empty array for "/")
      parameters - query-string and additional parameters extracted during parsing
      content - raw request body bytes (empty array if no body was present)

  • Method Details

    • getHttpCommand

      public String getHttpCommand()
      Returns the HTTP method from the request line.
      Returns:
      the HTTP command (e.g. "GET", "POST", "DELETE")

    • getUri

      public String getUri()
      Returns the full request URI as it appeared on the request line.
      Returns:
      the URI string, including the query string if one was present

    • getUriSegments

      public String[] getUriSegments()
      Returns the path portion of the URI split into segments.

      The leading slash is stripped before splitting. For a request to "/", an empty array is returned.

      Returns:
      an array of path segments (never null)

    • getParameters

      public Map<String,String> getParameters()
      Returns the parameters map extracted from the query string and, in course/test mode, from additional key=value lines after the headers.
      Returns:
      a Map of parameter names to values (never null); callers should treat it as read-only unless intentional mutation is required

    • getContent

      public byte[] getContent()
      Returns the raw bytes of the request body.
      Returns:
      the body content as a byte array (never null; empty array if no body was present)
